channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Oil prices fall as US-Iran peace negotiations ease concerns over Strait of Hormuz disruption
item.description: Bumaba ang presyo ng langis sa pandaigdigang merkado noong May 25 matapos lumakas ang pag-asa na maaaring magkaroon ng kasunduan sa pagitan ng Estados Unidos at Iran upang mabawasan ang tensyon sa Mid...

item.title: Aisha Yesufu Says Her Focus Na NDC Ticket And FCT Voters Ahead Of 2027 Election
item.description: Activist and FCT senatorial aspirant Aisha Yesufu don talk say she no see FCT Minister Nyesom Wike as her direct political opponent ahead of the 2027 general election. During interview for Sunday Poli...

item.title: UK set for record-breaking May heat as temperatures reach mid-30s
item.description: The United Kingdom is facing an exceptional spell of hot weather, with forecasters warning that Monday could bring the hottest May day on record. Temperatures are expected to climb as high as 34C, sur...

item.title: PTI leaders meet Interior Minister to review Khyber Pakhtunkhwa security situation
item.description: Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) has confirmed that Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Chief Minister Sohail Afridi and party chairman Barrister Gohar Ali Khan held a meeting with Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi to disc...

item.title: Bystander critically injured following fatal shooting near White House security checkpoint
item.description: According to a report by The Associated Press, a fatal shooting occurred near a security checkpoint close to the White House, leaving a bystander in serious condition. The incident prompted an immedia...

item.title: The turbulent final collaboration between Werner Herzog and Klaus Kinski during Cobra Verde (1987)
item.description: The relationship between filmmaker Werner Herzog and actor Klaus Kinski is widely regarded as one of the most intense and volatile partnerships in cinema history, reaching a particularly dramatic peak...

item.title: Centre ropes in IIT experts and public sector banks to stabilise CBSE post-result portal
item.description: The Union government has stepped in to address widespread technical issues reported on the Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) post-result re-evaluation portal, which has caused inconvenience ...

item.title: Ontario participants break Guinness World Record with 254-player human foosball match in Toronto
item.description: A group of 254 participants gathered at Humber Polytechnic’s Lakeshore Campus in Toronto to set a new Guinness World Record for the world’s largest game of human foosball. Despite heavy rain throughou...

item.title: Army Cheetah helicopter crash in Leh linked to engine failure, inquiry underway
item.description: A technical failure resulting in loss of engine power led to an Indian Army Cheetah helicopter crash in the mountainous Tangtse area near Leh on May 20, according to officials familiar with initial fi...

item.title: Article Examines the Historical Roots of US Sanctions and Political Pressure Against Cuba
item.description: The article argues that the long-standing US embargo and recent sanctions against Cuba are rooted not only in geopolitical rivalry, but also in opposition to Cuba’s socialist policies and internationa...

item.title: Rep. Thomas Massie says he will publicly identify individuals linked to Epstein files before leaving Congress
item.description: Outgoing Rep. Thomas Massie, a Republican from Kentucky who recently lost his re-election bid after seven terms in Congress, has pledged to continue his efforts to expose information related to Jeffre...

item.title: DA calls for stronger oversight and investment in KZN sports, arts and cultural development
item.description: An opinion piece by DA MPL Tammy Colley argues that KwaZulu-Natal’s sports, arts and culture sector requires far greater investment, oversight and accountability to unlock its social and economic pote...

item.title: Earlier atrial fibrillation screening recommended for Indigenous Australians to reduce stroke risk
item.description: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people face more than double the risk of stroke compared to non-Indigenous Australians, with strokes occurring at younger ages and leading to higher rates of fata...

item.title: Shooting Stars secure CAF Confederation Cup spot after 27 years away from continental football
item.description: Shooting Stars Sports Club (3SC) have finally secured a return to continental football after finishing third in the 2025/26 Nigeria Premier Football League season, ending a long 27-year absence. Despi...

item.title: A Look Back at Edwige Fenech’s Influence on 1970s Italian Cinema
item.description: The article published by Vintage Everyday celebrates the career and enduring legacy of Edwige Fenech, one of the most recognizable actresses in European cinema during the 1970s. Born in France and lat...

item.title: French prosecutors investigate death of child found with towel around neck
item.description: Authorities in France have opened a homicide investigation after a child was found dead with a towel wrapped around the neck, according to prosecutors. The incident reportedly happened in the Rennes a...

item.title: California authorities investigate animal rescue after dead dogs were discovered buried on property
item.description: Authorities in Northern California are investigating serious allegations of animal cruelty involving Miranda’s Rescue, an animal shelter based in Fortuna, Humboldt County. The investigation began afte...

item.title: Experts call for major reforms to reduce infrastructure cost blowouts in Australia
item.description: Australia continues to struggle with delivering major infrastructure projects on time and within budget, according to the article by Dominic D Ahiaga-Dagbui. The piece argues that repeated cost overru...

item.title: Kimi Antonelli secures fourth consecutive Formula 1 win after dramatic Canadian Grand Prix battle
item.description: Mercedes driver Kimi Antonelli continued his remarkable start to the Formula 1 season by winning the Canadian Grand Prix in Montreal, marking his fourth consecutive victory of the year. The 19-year-ol...

item.title: Poverty and weak schooling push Araria families in Bihar to send children to distant madrasas
item.description: The report highlights how extreme poverty, poor schooling infrastructure, and repeated natural hardships in Bihar’s Araria district are pushing families to send their children to madrasas located hund...
